name: Truliant
description_short: Truliant Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving 300,000+ members across NC, SC, and VA with $5B+ in assets since 1952.
description_long:
Truliant Federal Credit Union was chartered in 1952 to serve Western Electric employees in Winston-Salem, initially as Radio Shops Credit Union with just 2,000 members. The organization has grown substantially over seven decades, now operating more than 35 branches across North Carolina, South Carolina, and Virginia, and managing over $5 billion in assets. The credit union maintains its original mission rooted in the "people helping people" philosophy that defines the credit union movement.

Truliant offers comprehensive financial products including mortgages with rates advertised in the low 5s, auto refinancing starting at 3.99% APR, personal loans from 7.99% APR, and full-service banking with online and mobile platforms. Members can access prequalification and electronic closing (easyClose) for mortgages in North Carolina, along with traditional loan applications and account management. The institution provides educational resources through Money Burst (personal finance video content), financial wellness hubs, and guidance on credit improvement, debt payoff, and retirement planning.

As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, Truliant emphasizes member-first service, personalized advice, and competitive rates positioned as superior to larger national banks. The organization actively promotes community involvement and career opportunities, positioning itself around member financial goals rather than shareholder returns. Their longevity (70+ years) and substantial asset base ($5B+) indicate institutional stability and regulatory compliance within the federal credit union system.

The primary limitation is geographic restriction—membership and branch access are limited to North Carolina, South Carolina, and Virginia. The website content does not specify membership eligibility requirements beyond regional location, though historically credit unions required occupational or community affiliation. Online banking was temporarily unavailable during the review period, suggesting occasional service disruptions. Specific details on fees, minimum balances, and full product terms are not extensively detailed on the public homepage. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Auto refinancing loans (3.99% APR starting rate)
Mortgage loans with prequalification and electronic closing (NC only)
Financial wellness and education resources (Money Burst video series)
Credit improvement guidance and financial counseling
pros: Over $5 billion in assets and 300,000+ members provides institutional stability and NCUA insurance protection
Auto refinancing rates starting at 3.99% APR (2019-2026 vehicles, minimum $5K loan)
Electronic closing (easyClose) available for mortgages in North Carolina speeds up home purchase process
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits return to members through higher savings rates and lower loan rates
35+ branch locations across three states (NC, SC, VA) provides regional accessibility
Free financial education resources including Money Burst video series and financial wellness hub
70+ year operating history since 1952 demonstrates longevity and regulatory compliance
cons: Geographic limitation—only serves members in North Carolina, South Carolina, and Virginia; no nationwide online membership available
Online banking platform experiences scheduled maintenance downtime (was unavailable during website review)
easyClose electronic closing is North Carolina-only; other states require traditional closing methods
Membership eligibility requirements not clearly disclosed on public website; traditional credit union field-of-membership restrictions may apply
Limited details on fees, minimum account balances, and specific loan terms available on homepage
best_for: Regional residents in NC, SC, or VA seeking below-market auto refinancing rates on qualifying vehicles
Home buyers in North Carolina wanting simplified mortgage application and electronic closing options
Credit union members prioritizing member-owned institutional philosophy over for-profit bank structure
Consumers in Truliant's service area looking for personalized financial advice and community-focused lending
diagnosis: Truliant is best for existing or prospective residents in North Carolina, South Carolina, or Virginia seeking competitive credit union rates on auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages with member-owned institutional values. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to three states; national consumers or those outside this region cannot access membership or services, and membership eligibility requirements tied to location or affiliation may apply.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
